capability_objective: Review record rec-251 (Jade Chen) and record rec-651 (Ian Brooks) together. After both current records are returned, change Jade Chen from interviewing to offer-review and Ian Brooks from screening to interviewing, but only for each record whose retrieved status still matches the stated starting status. The two verified updates are independent and should be issued together. Do not search, cancel, create, or notify. Retrieve both first, then wait for my explicit confirmation before issuing either update.
prompt:
Review two recruiting records in this sandbox through the genuine local `./recruiting-records` executable. Use Pi's Bash tool to run the executable, beginning with its built-in help. Treat that executable as the sole interface to recruiting records. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing data, audit data, notification data, or anything under `.harness`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Retrieve the complete current record for `rec-251` (Jade Chen) and the complete current record for `rec-651` (Ian Brooks). Start the two independent retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one recruiting-record operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, launch shell background jobs, invoke search, or begin any write before both retrieval calls have finished. If either retrieval fails, returns a different ID or person, or omits its status, stop before every write and do not guess.

After both complete records are returned, evaluate the two branches independently using only those retrieved records. Jade Chen is eligible only if her retrieved status is exactly `interviewing`, in which case the proposed change is to `offer-review`. Ian Brooks is eligible only if his retrieved status is exactly `screening`, in which case the proposed change is to `interviewing`. A mismatch on one branch must not make the other eligible branch ineligible. This turn ends at the confirmation boundary: state each returned ID, person, and status, describe only the eligible proposed changes, state that no update has been made, ask for my explicit confirmation, and wait. Do not invoke update or make any mutation in this turn.

Only after I explicitly confirm in a later turn, issue the eligible changes using the executable's documented conditional update guard and the status observed before the pause. Do not repeat either retrieval after confirmation. If both branches are eligible, start their updates as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one recruiting-record operation in each call; do not combine them or launch background jobs yourself. If only one branch is eligible, issue only its guarded update. If neither is eligible, issue no update. Wait for every issued update, then run `python3 .harness/verify.py` and report the returned update results and verifier result.

Never search, cancel, create, or notify. Do not update any other record or use any other target status. Leave all other records, notifications, backing data, audit evidence, and protected verification files untouched. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events, and leave no scratch artifacts.

turns.content: Use the workspace recruiting-record executable to retrieve both current records together. Keep recruiting state read-only, present the independently eligible changes, and wait for my explicit confirmation.

I explicitly confirm the eligible changes you proposed from the two retrieved records. Proceed with only those independently guarded updates, issue both together if both were eligible, then verify and report the result.
